members.name: GetPlaybackPosition
members.summary: Returns the position in the [AudioStream] of the latest sound, in seconds. Returns [code]0.0[/code] if no sounds are playing. [b]Note:[/b] The position is not always accurate, as the [AudioServer] does not mix audio every processed frame. To get more accurate results, add [method AudioServer.get_time_since_last_mix] to the returned position. [b]Note:[/b] This method always returns [code]0.0[/code] if the [member stream] is an [AudioStreamInteractive], since it can have multiple clips playing at once.

members.name: GetStreamPlayback
members.summary: Returns the latest [AudioStreamPlayback] of this node, usually the most recently created by [method play]. If no sounds are playing, this method fails and returns an empty playback.

members.name: HasStreamPlayback
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if any sound is active, even if [member stream_paused] is set to [code]true[/code]. See also [member playing] and [method get_stream_playback].

members.name: Play
members.summary: Plays a sound from the beginning, or the given [param from_position] in seconds.

members.name: Seek
members.summary: Restarts all sounds to be played from the given [param to_position], in seconds. Does nothing if no sounds are playing.

members.name: Stop
members.summary: Stops all sounds from this node.

members.name: Autoplay
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this node calls [method play] when entering the tree.

members.name: Bus
members.summary: The target bus name. All sounds from this node will be playing on this bus. [b]Note:[/b] At runtime, if no bus with the given name exists, all sounds will fall back on [code]"Master"[/code]. See also [method AudioServer.get_bus_name].

members.name: MaxPolyphony
members.summary: The maximum number of sounds this node can play at the same time. Calling [method play] after this value is reached will cut off the oldest sounds.

members.name: MixTarget
members.summary: The mix target channels. Has no effect when two speakers or less are detected (see [enum AudioServer.SpeakerMode]).

members.name: PitchScale
members.summary: The audio's pitch and tempo, as a multiplier of the [member stream]'s sample rate. A value of [code]2.0[/code] doubles the audio's pitch, while a value of [code]0.5[/code] halves the pitch.

members.name: PlaybackType
members.summary: The playback type of the stream player. If set other than to the default value, it will force that playback type.

members.name: Playing
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this node is playing sounds. Setting this property has the same effect as [method play] and [method stop].

members.name: Stream
members.summary: The [AudioStream] resource to be played. Setting this property stops all currently playing sounds. If left empty, the [AudioStreamPlayer] does not work.

members.name: StreamPaused
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the sounds are paused. Setting [member stream_paused] to [code]false[/code] resumes all sounds. [b]Note:[/b] This property is automatically changed when exiting or entering the tree, or this node is paused (see [member Node.process_mode]).

members.name: VolumeDb
members.summary: Volume of sound, in decibels. This is an offset of the [member stream]'s volume. [b]Note:[/b] To convert between decibel and linear energy (like most volume sliders do), use [member volume_linear], or [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] and [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db].

members.name: VolumeLinear
members.summary: Volume of sound, as a linear value. [b]Note:[/b] This member modifies [member volume_db] for convenience. The returned value is equivalent to the result of [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] on [member volume_db]. Setting this member is equivalent to setting [member volume_db] to the result of [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db] on a value.

signals.name: Finished
signals.summary: Emitted when a sound finishes playing without interruptions. This signal is [i]not[/i] emitted when calling [method stop], or when exiting the tree while sounds are playing.

values.name: MIX_TARGET_CENTER
values.summary: The audio will be played on the second channel, which is usually the center.

values.name: MIX_TARGET_STEREO
values.summary: The audio will be played only on the first channel. This is the default.

values.name: MIX_TARGET_SURROUND
values.summary: The audio will be played on all surround channels.

constants.name: MIX_TARGET_STEREO
constants.summary: The audio will be played only on the first channel. This is the default.

constants.name: MIX_TARGET_SURROUND
constants.summary: The audio will be played on all surround channels.

constants.name: MIX_TARGET_CENTER
constants.summary: The audio will be played on the second channel, which is usually the center.
